sections.paragraph: [only in const. כִּבְרַת] f. pr. length (from כָּבַר No. 2), hence of a certain measure of distance, just as many other words denoting measure, weight, time, are used of certain measures, weights, and spaces of time (compare Heb. סְאָה, שֶׁקֶל, מָנֶה, יָמִים; Ch. עִדָּן a long time, specially a year, שָׁעָה, ܫܥܐܳ, ὥρα, a short time, specially an hour; Germ. Acker Land, ein Maaß Wein, Lat. pondo, whence Hfund). But what this measure may have been, cannot certainly be gathered from the occurrences, Gen 35:16 48:7 2Ki 5:19. The LXX. once ( Gen 48:7) add for the sake of explanation, ἱππόδρομος, which is either stadium (see Hody, De Bibl. Text. Originalibus, p. 115), or a measure used by the Arabs (شَوْطُ اُلْفَرَسِ) i.e. a distance such as a horse can go without being overworked; about three parasangs (eine Station), see Koehler ad Abulf. Syriam, p. 27.
